summary:
Pyrexia of Unknown Origin is defined by persistent high-grade fevers lasting over three weeks post-investigation; hence an incorrect criterion would be diagnosing it based solely upon one-week hospital investigation without full diagnostic evaluation as per standard guidelines.

The summary provided focuses too much on additional details and does not strictly adhere to keeping only two core sentences that capture essential information regarding defining characteristics versus non-characteristics within PYREXIA OF UNKNOWN ORIGIN. Let’s refine further while ensuring brevity but retaining critical elements about time frame vs other aspects excluded from classic definition.

Revised Summary: 
Pyrexia of Unknown Origin typically lasts longer than three weeks requiring extensive testing before diagnosis. An exception excludes short-term hospitalized evaluations concluding no cause immediately due to insufficient follow-up period fitting traditional criteria.
